- Fix `roomId` extraction from page source (https://github.com/zerodytrash/TikTok-Live-Connector/pull/212) by @fmalk

Update via `npm i tiktok-live-connector`

evidence.title: README (segment 1)
evidence.excerpt: A Node.js library to receive live stream events such as comments and gifts in realtime from [TikTok LIVE](https://www.tiktok.com/live) by connecting to TikTok's internal Webcast push service. This package includes a wrapper that connects to the Webcast service using just the username (`@uniqueId`). This allows you to connect to your own live chat as well as the live chat of other streamers. No credentials are required. Besides [Chat Comments](#chat), other events such as [Members Joining](#member), [Gifts](#gift), [Subscriptions](#subscribe), [Viewers](#roomuser), [Follows](#social), [Shares](#social), [Questions](#questionnew), [Likes](#like) and [Battles](#linkmicbattle) can be tracked.

readme.text:
# API BRICKS SDK ![GitHub language count](https://img.shields.io/github/languages/count/api-bricks/api-bricks-sdk) Welcome to the API BRICKS SDK. This repository contain SDK for our API's. ## Our Products This repository provides SDKs for our main product families: CoinAPI and FinFeedAPI. ### CoinAPI **Main Website:** [www.coinapi.io](https://www.coinapi.io/) **Documentation Hub:** [https://docs.coinapi.io/](https://docs.coinapi.io/) The CoinAPI family includes the following specific APIs and SDKs present in this repository: | Product Name | Description | Protocols | | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------- | | Market Data API | SDK for CoinAPI Market Data API | REST, WebSocket, FIX | | EMS Cloud Management SDK | SDK for CoinAPI Execution Management System Managed Cloud REST API | REST | | EMS Gateway REST SDK | SDK for CoinAPI Execution Management System REST API | REST, WS | | EMS Gateway FIX SDK | SDK for CoinAPI Execution Management System FIX API | FIX | | Indexes API | SDK for CoinAPI Indexes API | REST, WS | | Exchange Rates API | SDK for CoinAPI Exchange Rates API | REST, WS | | Flat Files API Processing Samples | Examples related to processing GZIP+CSV flat files | GZIP+CSV | *For detailed documentation on each specific API, please refer to the [CoinAPI Documentation Hub](https://docs.coinapi.io/).* ### FinFeedAPI **Main Website:** [www.finfeedapi.com](https://www.finfeedapi.com/) **Documentation Hub:** [https://docs.finfeedapi.com/](https://docs.finfeedapi.com/) The FinFeedAPI family includes the following specific APIs and SDKs present in this repository: | Product Name | Description | Protocols | | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | --------------- | | Stock API | SDK for FinFeed API Stock API | REST | | SEC API | SDK for FinFeed API SEC API | REST | | Currencies Realtime API | SDK for FinFeed API Currencies Realtime API | REST | | Currencies Historical API | SDK for FinFeed API Currencies Historical API | REST | | Prediction Markets API | SDK for FinFeed API Prediction Markets API | REST | *For detailed documentation on each specific API, please refer to the [FinFeedAPI Documentation Hub](https://docs.finfeedapi.com/).* ## Repository organization The repository is organized into two main product families, `coinapi` and `finfeedapi`. Each of these contains subdirectories for specific APIs and their associated SDKs or samples. ### CoinAPI Directories Directory | Description | --- | --- | `coinapi/market-data-api-rest` | SDK for CoinAPI Market Data REST API | `coinapi/market-data-api-ws` | SDK for CoinAPI Market Data WebSocket API | `coinapi/market-data-api-fix` | SDK for CoinAPI Market Data FIX API | `coinapi/ems-cloud-mgmt-api` | SDK for CoinAPI Execution Management System Managed Cloud REST API | `coinapi/ems-api-rest` | SDK for CoinAPI Execution Management System REST API | `coinapi/ems-api-fix` | SDK for CoinAPI Execution Management System FIX API | `coinapi/indexes-api-rest` | SDK for CoinAPI Indexes REST API | `coinapi/exchange-rates-api-historical` | SDK for CoinAPI Exchange Rates Historical REST API | `coinapi/exchange-rates-api-realtime` | SDK for CoinAPI Exchange Rates Realtime REST API (this might also cover WebSocket or be split, TBC) | `coinapi/exchange-rates-api-rest-historical` | SDK for CoinAPI Exchange Rates REST Historical API (potentially redundant or more specific version of `exchange-rates-api-historical`) | `coinapi/exchange-rates-api-rest-realtime` | SDK for CoinAPI Exchange Rates REST Realtime API (potentially redundant or more specific version of `exchange-rates-api-realtime`) | `coinapi/flat-files-api-processing-samples` | Examples related to processing GZIP+CSV flat files | ### FinFeedAPI Directories Directory | Description | --- | --- | `finfeedapi/stock-api-rest` | SDK for FinFeed API Stock REST API | `finfeedapi/sec-api-rest` | SDK for FinFeed API SEC REST API | `finfeedapi/currencies-api-rest-realtime` | SDK for FinFeed API Currencies Realtime REST API | `finfeedapi/currencies-api-rest-historical` | SDK for FinFeed API Currencies Historical REST API | `finfeedapi/prediction-markets-api-rest` | SDK for FinFeed API Prediction Markets REST API | ## OpenAPI Specifications OpenAPI (formerly Swagger) specification files, typically named `openapi.yaml` or `openapi.json`, are provided for many of the RESTful APIs in this repository. These files define the API's structure and can be used with various tools for documentation generation, client SDK creation, and testing. You can generally find these specification files within the respective API's directory. Common locations include: * A `spec/` subdirectory directly within the API's folder (e.g., `coinapi/market-data-api-rest/spec/openapi.yaml`). * Within language-specific SDK directories, often under an `api/` or similar subdirectory (e.g., `finfeedapi/sec-api-rest/sdk/java/api/openapi.yaml`). Please explore the individual API directories for the most up-to-date and specific locations of these files. ## Package managers coverage Language | Package manager | Protocol | Package name | Version/Link | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | C# | NuGet | REST | CoinAPI.REST.V1 | ![Nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/CoinAPI.REST.v1) | C# | NuGet | WebSocket | CoinAPI.WebSocket.V1 | ![Nuget](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/CoinAPI.WebSocket.v1) | Java | Maven
(GitHub Packages) | REST | io.coinapi.rest.v1 | https://github.com/api-bricks/api-bricks-sdk/packages/397337 | Java | Maven
(GitHub Packages) | WebSocket | io.coinapi.websocket.v1 | https://github.com/api-bricks/api-bricks-sdk/packages/397352 | Python3 | PyPI | REST | coinapi.rest.v1 | ![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/coinapi.rest.v1) | ## Language coverage In this repository you can find libraries that will help you use our API with minimal development effort from languages like: * Python * R * Matlab * C# * C++ * .NET * Java * Ruby * Go * JavaScript * TypeScript * Node.js * PHP * Haskell * Objective-C * Swift Feel free to make Pull Request with proposed changes or create an Issue on which we respond as soon as possible.

scout.summary: TikTok-Live-Connector is a popular TypeScript-based Node.js library that reverse-engineers TikTok's Webcast service to provide real-time access to live stream events like comments, gifts, and user joins without requiring credentials. While widely used and actively maintained, it is explicitly not production-ready and faces reliability issues due to its reliance on undocumented TikTok endpoints.
highlights: Enables real-time listening to TikTok LIVE events (comments, gifts, follows, etc.) using only a username.
No authentication or credentials required—connects via reverse-engineered Webcast push service.
Active ecosystem with rewrites in Python, Java, Go, and C#; strong community support (1.9K+ stars, Discord presence).
Clear disclaimer that this is a reverse engineering project, not suitable for production use.
risks: Frequent connection and data-fetching issues (e.g., missing chat messages, gift events not firing, 403 errors) indicate instability from reliance on unofficial APIs.
High-level users (gift level 25+) may not trigger expected 'member join' events, leading to incomplete audience tracking.
Subject to breaking changes from TikTok updates, as evidenced by past fixes for room data extraction and WebSocket handshake errors.
Not recommended for production environments per README warning; users directed to EulerStream’s official WebSocket API instead.

inventor.summary: TikTok-Live-Connector is a reverse-engineered Node.js library that enables real-time capture of TikTok LIVE events by interfacing directly with TikTok’s internal Webcast push service, using only a streamer’s username and no credentials. It serves as a foundational tool for developers building live interaction bots or analytics tools, though it is explicitly not production-ready.
highlights: Invents a credential-free method to tap into TikTok LIVE events (comments, gifts, joins, etc.) via reverse-engineered Webcast protocol.
Provides a simple TypeScript API that abstracts low-level connection logic, enabling rapid prototyping for live chat applications.
Sparked a multi-language ecosystem of rewrites (Python, Java, Go, C#), indicating strong inventive influence across developer communities.
Includes built-in support for dynamic room discovery and event routing without requiring session tokens or cookies.
risks: Core functionality depends on undocumented and volatile TikTok endpoints, making the invention fragile to platform changes.
Critical event gaps (e.g., missing high-level user joins, inconsistent gift triggers) undermine reliability for interactive use cases.
Lack of official support or stable contracts increases maintenance burden on adopters who must track upstream fixes.
Not designed for scale or uptime—unsuitable for commercial deployments per author’s own guidance.

engineer.summary: TikTok-Live-Connector is a TypeScript-based Node.js library that reverse-engineers TikTok's Webcast service to deliver real-time live stream events without authentication. While it offers a clean API for prototyping and has spurred multi-language ports, it suffers from reliability issues due to its dependence on unstable, undocumented endpoints and is explicitly discouraged for production use.
highlights: Provides a lightweight, credential-free interface to TikTok LIVE events (comments, gifts, joins, etc.) using only a username.
Built in TypeScript with modular structure (src/, test/, tsconfig) and modern tooling (Vitest, Prettier, Babel).
Actively maintained with recent fixes for room data extraction, WebSocket handshake errors, and signing server flexibility.
Serves as a reference implementation inspiring official-looking rewrites in Python, Java, Go, and C#.
risks: Event loss and inconsistency (e.g., missing chat messages, gift events, or high-level user joins) undermine data integrity for real-time applications.
Frequent HTTP 403 errors and connection drops indicate fragility against TikTok’s anti-scraping measures or protocol changes.
Not production-ready per README; users are redirected to EulerStream’s commercial WebSocket API for reliable usage.
Reliance on page scraping for roomId and dynamic signing servers introduces additional points of failure.

skeptic.summary: TikTok-Live-Connector presents itself as a convenient tool for real-time TikTok LIVE event monitoring, but its foundation on reverse-engineered, undocumented protocols introduces severe reliability and sustainability concerns that outweigh its ease-of-use claims.
highlights: Advertises broad event coverage (comments, gifts, joins, etc.) but multiple open issues confirm critical events are frequently missed or inconsistent.
Explicitly disclaimed as 'not production-ready' yet widely adopted—raising questions about user awareness of its fragility.
Dependence on scraping and volatile internal APIs makes it inherently vulnerable to TikTok countermeasures or minor UI/backend changes.
Despite active maintenance, recurring connection failures and data-fetching errors (e.g., 403s, missing room data) persist across versions.
risks: Users may build applications assuming reliable event delivery, only to encounter silent data loss (e.g., high-level users not triggering join events).
The library’s core functionality could break entirely with any TikTok protocol update, with no recourse beyond waiting for community patches.
Promotion of unofficial access methods may expose users to terms-of-service violations or IP-based blocking by TikTok.
Reliance on third-party signing servers (introduced in v1.1.9) adds external dependencies that could become unavailable or compromised.

scout.summary: The repository provides SDKs for two main product families—CoinAPI and FinFeedAPI—with support for multiple protocols including REST, WebSocket, and FIX. It is actively maintained with a moderate number of stars and forks, but has open issues and no formal releases.
highlights: SDKs cover diverse financial data APIs including Market Data, EMS, Indexes, Exchange Rates, and Flat Files processing.
Supports multiple communication protocols (REST, WebSocket, FIX, GZIP+CSV) indicating flexibility for different integration needs.
Documentation is centralized via external hubs for both CoinAPI and FinFeedAPI.
risks: Presence of open bugs (e.g., in limitbook_full_to_snapshot_50.py) may affect reliability.
No GitHub releases suggest potential instability or lack of versioned distribution.
Limited issue activity (only 2 issues total) might indicate low community engagement or underreporting.

inventor.summary: The repository invents and organizes SDK toolkits for two distinct financial API product families—CoinAPI and FinFeedAPI—each with specialized modules supporting multiple communication protocols and data formats, enabling developers to integrate diverse market data and trading functionalities.
highlights: Invents modular SDKs grouped under CoinAPI and FinFeedAPI, each targeting specific financial data services like Market Data, EMS, Indexes, and Exchange Rates.
Introduces protocol-agnostic design by supporting REST, WebSocket, FIX, and GZIP+CSV across different SDK components, enhancing interoperability.
Structures the codebase with clear product-family directories (coinapi, finfeedapi, mcp) aligned with documented API offerings.
risks: Lack of versioned releases may hinder reproducibility and stable adoption of invented SDK components.
An open bug in a core data processing script (limitbook_full_to_snapshot_50.py) suggests potential flaws in invented data transformation logic.
No evidence of automated tests or examples beyond flat-file samples may limit confidence in the correctness of invented SDK behaviors.

engineer.summary: The repository provides well-structured, multi-protocol SDKs for CoinAPI and FinFeedAPI, organized into clear product-family directories. It supports diverse integration needs via REST, WebSocket, FIX, and GZIP+CSV, but lacks versioned releases and has an open bug in a core data processing script, raising concerns about stability and test coverage.
highlights: Codebase is cleanly organized with top-level directories for coinapi, finfeedapi, and mcp, matching the documented product families and SDK offerings.
SDKs support multiple protocols (REST, WebSocket, FIX, GZIP+CSV), enabling flexible integration patterns for market data, trading, and file-based workflows.
External documentation hubs are referenced consistently, reducing README clutter and centralizing API specifications.
risks: Open bug in limitbook_full_to_snapshot_50.py (E-IS-002) indicates potential issues in critical order book processing logic without a known fix.
Absence of GitHub releases (confirmed via repo_profile.releases_count=0 and root_files evidence) complicates dependency management and production deployment.
No visible test suites or CI configuration in root files (E-RF-001) suggests insufficient validation of SDK behavior across protocols and edge cases.

skeptic.summary: While the repository claims to provide robust, multi-protocol SDKs for CoinAPI and FinFeedAPI, critical gaps in release management, unresolved bugs, and lack of visible testing infrastructure undermine confidence in its production readiness and long-term reliability.
highlights: The repository bundles SDKs for two distinct API families but offers no versioned releases, making it difficult to assess stability or track changes over time.
An open bug in a core data processing script (limitbook_full_to_snapshot_50.py) remains unaddressed, suggesting potential neglect of critical functionality.
Despite claims of supporting multiple protocols (REST, WebSocket, FIX, GZIP+CSV), there is no evidence of test suites or CI pipelines to validate cross-protocol correctness.
risks: Absence of GitHub releases (releases_count=0) implies ad-hoc distribution, increasing integration risk for dependent projects.
The open issue E-IS-002 points to a defect in order book snapshot generation—a core financial data operation—without resolution or workaround.
Root files show no test directories or configuration files (e.g., .github/workflows, pytest.ini), raising doubts about code quality and maintenance rigor.
